title.text: Language and translation options on your platform

body:0001.text: What are language options on the platform?

body:0002.text: Understand how language settings shape the user experience and make your platform accessible to multilingual communities.

body:0003.text: Your platform can support multiple languages. You control:

body:0004.text: The default platform language shown to first-time visitors.

body:0005.text: The languages users can choose to interact with.

body:0006.text: Whether content is translated manually or through automatic translation tools.

body:0007.text: Supporting multiple languages helps you engage a broader audience, improve inclusivity, and increase participation.

body:0009.text: Contact support via the chat bubble on your platform.

body:0010.text: Access depends on your subscription plan.

body:0012.text: Weglot: Translates the entire frontend UI and user-generated content.

body:0013.text: Google Translate: Translates only ideas and comments.

body:0015.text: Weglot is an add-on that auto-translates everything on your platform’s interface.

body:0016.text: A language switcher appears in the bottom-left of the screen.

body:0017.text: Weglot translates all visible content (UI, posts, project titles, etc.).

body:0018.text: Supports up to 3 languages by default (extendable to 10 upon agreement).

body:0020.text: To link directly to a specific language version, append ?lang=xx to any URL.

body:0023.text: ℹ️ The language must already be configured in Weglot for the link to work.

body:0025.text: Google translate is a lightweight translation of ideas, comments, and proposals without translating the full interface.

body:0028.text: Ideas and project input: also via "Translate" option

body:0031.text: Does not translate admin content or project descriptions.

body:0032.text: Assumes the user writes in their chosen default language — it won't detect and translate mismatched input.

body:0033.text: Does not support automatic translation of user profile language mismatches.

body:0034.text: To fix incorrect or incomplete translations:

body:0036.text: Use the language toggle to enter your own version in other languages.

body:0037.text: How to change the default language ?

body:0039.text: Make sure your preferred default language appears at the first language, on the far left of the language boxes.

body:0040.text: To adjust, drag or delete other languages until the correct one is in the first position.

body:0041.text: The default language determines the first impression visitors receive, and the base language used for account registration.

body:0042.text: How to add a new language?

body:0044.text: Click the dropdown arrow to view the full list of available languages.

body:0045.text: Select the languages you want to add.

body:0047.text: Adding a language does NOT auto-translate content, multiple languages need to be maintained manually

body:0048.text: When publishing a project: You must enter a translated title for each added language (required to publish). Descriptions can be added per language but are not required to publish, though strongly recommended.

body:0049.text: You cannot hide a project in specific languages. All added platform languages will show the project, even if translations are missing.

body:0051.text: Each user controls their own platform language preference.

body:0052.text: The language a user registers with becomes their default platform language.

body:0053.text: Users can change their preferred language anytime by clicking on their profile > My Settings > Language.

body:0054.text: This preference affects the UI and how auto-translation is applied to ideas/comments.
